thesis: Issuer of USDC, the second-largest stablecoin (~$75B market cap, 25% share). Revenue of $2.75B in FY2025 (+64% YoY) comes almost entirely from interest on Treasury-backed reserves managed by BlackRock. IPO'd June 2025 at $31, peaked ~$299. Revenue directly tied to interest rates and USDC supply growth. GENIUS Act passage is a major catalyst. Coinbase revenue-sharing (50% of non-Coinbase USDC interest) is the biggest structural drag on margins.

drivers: GENIUS Act creates favorable regulatory moat
New revenue streams (Circle Payments Network, CCTP fees)

risks: Interest rates fall below 2% — revenue collapses
USDC loses share to Tether or Ethena
Regulatory reversal or unfavorable stablecoin legislation

base_case.assumptions: USDC supply grows to $100B+ by 2027; interest rates stabilize 3–4%; GENIUS Act implemented; Coinbase share stays manageable; new revenue streams contribute 5–10%.

catalysts.event: MiCA compliance driving EU USDC adoption

key_risks: Interest rate sensitivity — revenue directly tied to Fed policy
Competition from Tether, bank-issued stablecoins, Ethena
Regulatory uncertainty despite GENIUS Act progress
